lca_software_and_database:
For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program SimaPro 9.1.1.1 (PRé Consultants, 2021) has been used in combination with a specific LCA software program for ETEX.

The main LCI source used in this study is the Ecoinvent 3.6 database (Wernet et al., 2019).

System boundaries (parsed from PDF diagram — no AI)

Declared modules (☒): A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, C1, C2, C3, C4, D
Not declared (☐): —
By life-cycle stage:

Use stage: B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7
End of life stage: C1, C2, C3, C4
Beyond the system boundaries: D

Module labels (vertical text):
A1: materials
A2: Transport
A3: Manufacturing
A4: Transport
A5: installation
B1: Use
B2: Maintenance
B3: Repair
B4: Replacement
B5: Refurbishment
B6: use
B7: use

C2: Transport
C3: processing
C4: Disposal
D: Recovery
production_sites: The production site is located at 59269 Neubeckum, Germany.
cut_off_criteria:
Following processes were considered below the cut-off:
• Transport of packaging of raw materials
• The metal templates in which the boards are produced need to be greased periodically. The template oil is considered below cut-off
• Packaging and transport of ancillary materials used during installation
• Waste treatment of ancillary materials used during installation
• Wearable sieves and cutting knives
allocation: At Etex, different types of cement fiber products are produced. However EQUITONE [natura-textura-materia] products are produced only on certain production lines, with no co-products being produced as part of the process. Only facility level data were available for electricity use, the use of natural gas, etc. The facility level data have been allocated to the analyzed product using their respective annual production volume (physical relationship), therefore volume allocation is applied. Material inputs and outputs which were not available at the product level, such as waste, were allocated similarly, by mass allocation.
data_quality:
The data used for the LCA are specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.

Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2016.

Company specific data for the product stage have been collected by Eternit and were provided to VITO through an excel file. The LCI data has been checked by the EPD verifier (Evert Vermaut, Vinçotte). VITO uses publicly available generic data for all background processes such as the production of electricity, transportation by means of a specific truck, etc. Primary data is used for modules A1, A2, A3, and A5. The rest of the study is based on scenarios (module A4, modules B1-B7, modules C1-C4, and module D).
time_representativeness: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2016.
geographical_representativeness: The German electricity mix has been used to model electricity use in life cycle stage A3. The Belgian electricity mix (consumption mix + import) has been used to model electricity use in life cycle stages A5, C1, C3, C4 and D. The used records are respectively the ecoinvent records 'Electricity, medium voltage {DE}| market for | Cut-off, U' and 'Electricity, low voltage {BE}| market for | Cutoff, U' (Wernet et al., 2016).
technology_representativeness: The data used for the LCA are specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.
impact_assessment: The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied. No additional or deviating characterization factors were used.
additional_paragraphs:
Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
• Infrastructure and land use of the factory
• Environmental impacts caused by the personnel of the production plants are not included in the LCA, e.g. waste from the cafeteria and sanitary installations, accidental pollution caused by human mistakes, or environmental effects caused by commuter traffic

The fibre cement panels contain cellulose, which is a biobased material. Uptake of biogenic CO2 within cellulose is reported in module A1, release of biogenic CO2 related to this flow is reported in C4.
The fibre cement panels are transported using wooden pallets and a carton coverage. Uptake of biogenic CO2 within these pallets and carton is reported in module A3, release in module A5.

Biogenic carbon content (kg C / FU)
Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate) 1,53E-01
Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate) 3,29E-01
Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804 and hence not taken into account in the calculations.

subsections.title: SOFTWARE
subsections.content: For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program SimaPro 9.1.1.1 (PRé Consultants, 2021) has been used in combination with a specific LCA software program for ETEX.

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON ALLOCATION
subsections.content: At Etex, different types of cement fiber products are produced. However EQUITONE [natura-textura-materia] products are produced only on certain production lines, with no co-products being produced as part of the process. Only facility level data were available for electricity use, the use of natural gas, etc. The facility level data have been allocated to the analyzed product using their respective annual production volume (physical relationship), therefore volume allocation is applied. Material inputs and outputs which were not available at the product level, such as waste, were allocated similarly, by mass allocation.

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON CUT OFF
subsections.content:
Following processes were considered below the cut-off:
• Transport of packaging of raw materials
• The metal templates in which the boards are produced need to be greased periodically. The template oil is considered below cut-off
• Packaging and transport of ancillary materials used during installation
• Waste treatment of ancillary materials used during installation
• Wearable sieves and cutting knives

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON EXCLUDED PROCESSES
subsections.content:
Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
• Infrastructure and land use of the factory
• Environmental impacts caused by the personnel of the production plants are not included in the LCA, e.g. waste from the cafeteria and sanitary installations, accidental pollution caused by human mistakes, or environmental effects caused by commuter traffic

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON BIOGENIC CARBON MODELLING
subsections.content:
The fibre cement panels contain cellulose, which is a biobased material. Uptake of biogenic CO2 within cellulose is reported in module A1, release of biogenic CO2 related to this flow is reported in C4.
The fibre cement panels are transported using wooden pallets and a carton coverage. Uptake of biogenic CO2 within these pallets and carton is reported in module A3, release in module A5.

Biogenic carbon content (kg C / FU)
Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate) 1,53E-01
Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate) 3,29E-01

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON CARBON OFFSETTING
subsections.content: Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804 and hence not taken into account in the calculations.

subsections.title: ADDITIONAL OR DEVIATING CHARACTERISATION FACTORS
subsections.content: The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied. No additional or deviating characterization factors were used.

subsections.title: SPECIFICITY
subsections.content: The data used for the LCA are specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.

subsections.title: PERIOD OF DATA COLLECTION
subsections.content: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2016.

subsections.title: INFORMATION ON DATA COLLECTION
subsections.content: Company specific data for the product stage have been collected by Eternit and were provided to VITO through an excel file. The LCI data has been checked by the EPD verifier (Evert Vermaut, Vinçotte). VITO uses publicly available generic data for all background processes such as the production of electricity, transportation by means of a specific truck, etc. Primary data is used for modules A1, A2, A3, and A5. The rest of the study is based on scenarios (module A4, modules B1-B7, modules C1-C4, and module D).

subsections.title: DATABASE USED FOR BACKGROUND DATA
subsections.content: The main LCI source used in this study is the Ecoinvent 3.6 database (Wernet et al., 2019).

subsections.title: ENERGY MIX
subsections.content: The German electricity mix has been used to model electricity use in life cycle stage A3. The Belgian electricity mix (consumption mix + import) has been used to model electricity use in life cycle stages A5, C1, C3, C4 and D. The used records are respectively the ecoinvent records 'Electricity, medium voltage {DE}| market for | Cut-off, U' and 'Electricity, low voltage {BE}| market for | Cutoff, U' (Wernet et al., 2016).
